tracks.title: Little Learners
tracks.checkpointPrompt: Finish the mission, then explain what your code is repeating or changing.

lessons.title: Meet the Turtle
lessons.description: Tell the turtle where to go and watch your first line appear.
lessons.mission: Make one bright path and add a short message.
lessons.visualGoal: A single line with a happy word at the end.
microHints: Then add a message with write("Hi", 22)

milestones.title: Make a trail
milestones.description: Draw at least one line using move(...).
milestones.hint: Try `move(120)`.

milestones.title: Say hello
milestones.description: Write a short message at the end of your trail.
milestones.hint: Try `write("Hi!", 22)` after your move.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make one bright path and add a short message.
milestones.hint: Move first, then write.

reward.title: Trailblazer Badge
reward.flavor: You made your very first turtle trail.

lessons.hint: Start with move(120), then try write("Hi!", 22).
lessons.concept: Commands run from top to bottom in order.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("skyblue")
move(120)
write("Hi!", 22)
lessons.successMessage: Nice work. You made the turtle move and added your own message.

lessons.title: Long and Short Lines
lessons.description: Change the number inside move(...) to make lines of different lengths.
lessons.mission: Draw at least three lines, and make one of them longer than the others.
lessons.visualGoal: A group of lines with clearly different lengths.
targetSteps: Try a small number and a bigger number
microHints: Start with a small move: move(40)

milestones.title: Make 3 lines
milestones.description: Draw at least three lines.
milestones.hint: Try move, turn, move, turn, move.

milestones.title: Add corners
milestones.description: Use turn(...) so your lines are not all on top of each other.
milestones.hint: Try `turn(120)` between moves.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw at least three lines, and make one of them longer than the others.
milestones.hint: Try a small number (40) and a bigger number (140).

reward.title: Length Explorer Badge
reward.flavor: You discovered that numbers change how far the turtle moves.

lessons.hint: Try move(40), move(90), and move(140) with little turns in between.
lessons.concept: Numbers inside commands change what the turtle does.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("royalblue")
move(40)
turn(120)
move(90)
turn(120)
move(140)
lessons.successMessage: Great job. You used different numbers to make different line lengths.

lessons.title: Turn Left, Turn Right
lessons.description: Use turns to make corners, bends, and zigzags.
lessons.mission: Make a zigzag path with lots of corners.
lessons.visualGoal: A back-and-forth zigzag path.
targetSteps: Repeat the bend a few times

milestones.title: Make the first bend
milestones.description: Draw a short line, turn, and draw another line.
milestones.hint: Try `move(60)`, then `turn(60)`, then `move(60)`.

milestones.title: Zigzag time
milestones.description: Make a zigzag with lots of corners.
milestones.hint: Alternate turns like `60` and `-120`.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a zigzag path with lots of corners.
milestones.hint: Move, turn, move, turn, repeat.

reward.title: Turn Tamer Badge
reward.flavor: You learned how direction changes the next line.

lessons.hint: Try move(60), turn(60), move(60), turn(-120), then keep going.
lessons.concept: Turning changes the direction of the next line.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("coral")
line_width(5)
move(60)
turn(60)
move(60)
turn(-120)
move(60)
turn(60)
move(60)
lessons.successMessage: Zigzag success. You used turns to guide the turtle in new directions.

lessons.title: Draw a Square
lessons.description: Put move and turn together to make a box shape.
lessons.mission: Draw a square with four equal sides.
lessons.visualGoal: A neat square with four matching sides.
targetSteps: Move the same amount each time
Turn a quarter-turn after every side
microHints: Try one side: move(90) then turn(90)
If you know repeat(...), use it

milestones.title: Make a corner
milestones.description: Draw one side and make one clean turn.
milestones.hint: Try `move(90)` then `turn(90)`.

milestones.title: Do it 4 times
milestones.description: Make 4 sides. Using repeat(4): is the easiest way.
milestones.hint:
Try:
repeat(4):
    move(90)
    turn(90)

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw a square with four equal sides.
milestones.hint: Same move each time, same turn each time.

reward.title: Corner Captain Badge
reward.flavor: You taught the turtle how to make clean corners.

lessons.hint: A square can be made with repeat(4): move(90) turn(90).
lessons.concept: A shape is made by combining distance and turning.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("tomato")
line_width(6)
repeat(4):
    move(90)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: Square complete. You used the same move and turn to close the shape.

lessons.title: Repeat Magic
lessons.description: Use repeat(...) so the turtle does the same job again and again.
lessons.mission: Draw a repeated shape without copying the same line of code over and over.
lessons.visualGoal: A shape made with a loop instead of lots of repeated lines.
targetSteps: Put move and turn inside the loop
microHints: Then turn(60) so it goes around

milestones.title: Start a loop
milestones.description: Use repeat(...) with an indented block.
milestones.hint:
Try:
repeat(6):
    move(60)
    turn(60)

milestones.title: Go all the way around
milestones.description: Make a full loop shape with lots of sides.
milestones.hint: Repeat 6 times and turn 60 each time.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw a repeated shape without copying the same line of code over and over.
milestones.hint: One loop can make a whole shape.

reward.title: Loop Starter Badge
reward.flavor: You used a loop to make the turtle do magic repeats.

lessons.hint: Try repeat(6): move(60) turn(60).
lessons.concept: Loops repeat instructions so you write less code.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("goldenrod")
line_width(4)
repeat(6):
    move(60)
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: Loop magic unlocked. One loop made a whole shape.

lessons.title: Triangles and Hexagons
lessons.description: Change the repeat count and turn amount to make different shapes.
lessons.mission: Draw a triangle and then a hexagon.
lessons.visualGoal: Two different loop-made shapes on the page.

milestones.title: Make a triangle
milestones.description: Draw a 3-sided triangle with a loop.
milestones.hint:
Try:
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)

milestones.title: Hop to a new spot
milestones.description: Move without drawing a line between shapes.
milestones.hint: Use `pen_up()`, `go_to(x, y)`, then `pen_down()`.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw a triangle and then a hexagon.
milestones.hint: Hexagon turn is 60 degrees (360 / 6).

reward.title: Shape Switcher Badge
reward.flavor: You discovered that loops can build different polygons.

lessons.hint: Try a triangle first, then lift the pen, move, and try a hexagon.
lessons.concept: Changing a loop changes the shape it creates.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("salmon")
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(340, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("cornflowerblue")
repeat(6):
    move(55)
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: You made two different polygons by changing the loop.

lessons.title: Pattern Spinner
lessons.description: Spin a shape a little each time to make a bigger pattern.
lessons.mission: Make a pattern by drawing a shape, turning, and drawing it again.
lessons.visualGoal: A spinning shape pattern that grows around the center.
targetSteps: Draw one shape with a loop
microHints: Make a small shape loop first (like a square)

milestones.title: Make one shape
milestones.description: Use a loop to draw a shape (like a square).
milestones.hint:
Try:
repeat(4):
    move(50)
    turn(90)

milestones.title: Spin it
milestones.description: Turn a little and draw the shape again to make a pattern.
milestones.hint: Put your shape loop inside another repeat(...), then `turn(45)`.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a pattern by drawing a shape, turning, and drawing it again.
milestones.hint: Keep the turn between shapes small (like 30–60).

reward.title: Pattern Spinner Badge
reward.flavor: You turned one shape into a whole pattern.

lessons.hint: Try a loop inside another repeat, then turn between each shape.
lessons.concept: Repeating a repeated shape creates bigger patterns.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("coral")
line_width(3)
repeat(8):
    repeat(4):
        move(50)
        turn(90)
    turn(45)
lessons.successMessage: Pattern spinner complete. You repeated a whole shape to make art.

lessons.title: Color Changes
lessons.description: Change the pen color to make your pattern brighter and more interesting.
lessons.mission: Make a repeating drawing with at least three colors.
lessons.visualGoal: A colorful pattern with obvious color changes.
microHints: Draw a little shape with repeat(...)

milestones.title: First color
milestones.description: Set a pen color and draw something.
milestones.hint: Try `pen_color("tomato")` before you draw.

milestones.title: Three colors
milestones.description: Use at least three different pen colors.
milestones.hint: Call pen_color(...) again before the next drawing.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a repeating drawing with at least three colors.
milestones.hint: Repeat makes the shape. Color makes it pop.

reward.title: Color Spark Badge
reward.flavor: You added color changes like a coding artist.

lessons.hint: Try drawing the same little shape again in a new color.
lessons.concept: Commands can change the turtle state, including color.
lessons.starterCode:
line_width(4)
pen_color("tomato")
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(260, 180)
pen_down()
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(360, 180)
pen_down()
pen_color("teal")
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: Color success. You used multiple colors to make your drawing pop.

lessons.title: Pen Up, Pen Down
lessons.description: Learn how to move without drawing, then start drawing again.
lessons.mission: Draw one shape, hop to a new place, and draw another shape.
lessons.visualGoal: Two shapes with a gap between them and no line connecting the gap.
targetSteps: Put the pen down and draw again
microHints: Draw one shape first (a square is great)

milestones.title: Draw the first shape
milestones.description: Draw one shape anywhere on the canvas.
milestones.hint: A square works: repeat(4): move(50) turn(90)

milestones.title: Hop without drawing
milestones.description: Use pen_up + go_to + pen_down so you don't draw a travel line.
milestones.hint: Try: pen_up(); go_to(340, 210); pen_down()

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw one shape, hop to a new place, and draw another shape.
milestones.hint: If you see a line between shapes, your pen is still down while moving.

reward.title: Hop Scout Badge
reward.flavor: You learned how to move the turtle without leaving a trail.

lessons.hint: Use pen_up(), go_to(...), and pen_down() between shapes.
lessons.concept: The turtle can move without drawing when the pen is up.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("cornflowerblue")
repeat(4):
    move(50)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(340, 210)
pen_down()
pen_color("coral")
repeat(3):
    move(60)
    turn(120)
lessons.successMessage: You used pen control to move without drawing between shapes.

lessons.title: Move to a New Spot
lessons.description: Place your drawings in new places on the canvas.
lessons.mission: Draw at least two shapes in different spots on the page.
lessons.visualGoal: Shapes placed in clearly different parts of the canvas.
microHints: Place shape 1 with: pen_up(); go_to(150, 120); pen_down()
Draw a small shape with repeat(...)
Place shape 2 with go_to(360, 220) and draw again

milestones.title: Place the first shape
milestones.description: Use go_to(...) to choose where your first shape goes.
milestones.hint: Try: pen_up(); go_to(150, 120); pen_down()

milestones.title: Place another shape
milestones.description: Draw a second shape after moving to a new spot.
milestones.hint: Go to a different corner and draw again.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw at least two shapes in different spots on the page.
milestones.hint: You can reuse the same shape twice. Placement is the point.

reward.title: Map Maker Badge
reward.flavor: You started placing art exactly where you wanted it.

lessons.hint: Try go_to(140, 120) for one shape and go_to(360, 220) for another.
lessons.concept: Coordinates let you place drawing elements on purpose.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_up()
go_to(150, 120)
pen_down()
pen_color("salmon")
repeat(3):
    move(70)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(360, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("cornflowerblue")
repeat(4):
    move(55)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: Nice placement. You used the canvas like a map.

lessons.title: Build a Tiny Scene
lessons.description: Put several shapes together to make one picture.
lessons.mission: Make a tiny scene with at least three parts, like a house, sun, and ground.
lessons.visualGoal: A simple scene with multiple shapes working together.
targetSteps: Place them so they feel like one picture
microHints: Start with one big shape (the house)
Add a roof or ground line
Add one more detail (sun, tree, or flower)

milestones.title: Make the main thing
milestones.description: Draw one main shape for your scene (like a house body).
milestones.hint: A square or rectangle is a great start.

milestones.title: Add two details
milestones.description: Add two more parts (roof, sun, ground, door, etc.).
milestones.hint: Use pen_up/go_to to place details without messy travel lines.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a tiny scene with at least three parts, like a house, sun, and ground.
milestones.hint: One big shape + two small shapes = a scene.

reward.title: Scene Starter Badge
reward.flavor: You turned separate shapes into one little world.

lessons.hint: Think of one big shape and then two smaller helpers around it.
lessons.concept: Several small shapes can combine into one scene.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_up()
go_to(190, 210)
pen_down()
pen_color("tomato")
repeat(4):
    move(80)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(190, 210)
pen_down()
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(380, 90)
pen_down()
pen_color("gold")
repeat(12):
    move(20)
    turn(30)
lessons.successMessage: Scene complete. You combined several shapes into one picture.

lessons.title: Layer Shapes
lessons.description: Draw shapes in an order that makes the picture feel deeper and fuller.
lessons.mission: Make a scene with overlapping or layered shapes in at least two colors.
lessons.visualGoal: A scene where one shape sits in front of or behind another.
targetSteps: Add another shape over or near it
microHints: Draw a big background shape first
Then draw a smaller shape on top of it
Change colors so the layers stand out

milestones.title: Background first
milestones.description: Draw one big shape first (the background).
milestones.hint: A big square/rectangle works great.

milestones.title: Put something in front
milestones.description: Draw another shape after the background so it looks on top.
milestones.hint: Use go_to(...) to place a smaller square/triangle on the big one.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a scene with overlapping or layered shapes in at least two colors.
milestones.hint: Change colors between layers so it feels like depth.

reward.title: Layer Artist Badge
reward.flavor: You learned that drawing order changes the whole picture.

lessons.hint: Try a big background shape first and smaller shapes after it.
lessons.concept: Drawing order changes what appears in front and behind.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_up()
go_to(160, 170)
pen_down()
pen_color("skyblue")
repeat(4):
    move(140)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(210, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("tomato")
repeat(4):
    move(70)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(330, 110)
pen_down()
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(12):
    move(18)
    turn(30)
lessons.successMessage: Layering success. You used order and color to make the scene richer.

lessons.title: Make a Stamp
lessons.description: Create your own drawing command with def so you can reuse it.
lessons.mission: Make a stamp() function that draws a square, then call it once.
lessons.visualGoal: A square drawn by a helper function you created.
microHints: Indent the square code under it (4 spaces)

milestones.title: Define stamp()
milestones.description: Create a function named stamp that draws a square.
milestones.hint:
Try:
def stamp():
    repeat(4):
        move(60)
        turn(90)

milestones.title: Use your stamp
milestones.description: Call stamp() at least once.
milestones.hint: On a new line: stamp()

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a stamp() function that draws a square, then call it once.
milestones.hint: If Python complains about indentation, make sure the square lines are indented.

reward.title: Stamp Maker Badge
reward.flavor: You made your first helper function.

lessons.hint: Define stamp() first. Then call stamp().
lessons.concept: Functions let you name a chunk of code and reuse it.
lessons.starterCode:
def stamp():
    repeat(4):
        move(60)
        turn(90)

stamp()
lessons.successMessage: Stamp complete. You made your own command with def and used it.

lessons.title: Stamp Parade
lessons.description: Reuse your stamp in new places without rewriting the square.
lessons.mission: Call stamp() three times in different places.
lessons.visualGoal: Three squares stamped around the canvas.
microHints: Repeat those hops two more times

milestones.title: Keep stamp() around
milestones.description: Define stamp() so you can reuse it.
milestones.hint: Use your stamp() from last lesson.

milestones.title: Hop to a new spot
milestones.description: Use pen_up + go_to + pen_down between stamps.
milestones.hint: Try: pen_up(); go_to(320, 220); pen_down()

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Call stamp() three times in different places.
milestones.hint: Call stamp(), hop, stamp(), hop, stamp().

reward.title: Stamp Parade Badge
reward.flavor: You reused one function to make three shapes.

lessons.hint: Define stamp() once, then call stamp() three times with hops in between.
lessons.concept: Reusing a function keeps your code short and easy to change.
lessons.starterCode:
def stamp():
    repeat(4):
        move(60)
        turn(90)

stamp()
pen_up()
go_to(340, 220)
pen_down()
stamp()
pen_up()
go_to(480, 160)
pen_down()
stamp()
lessons.successMessage: Stamp parade complete. One function, three shapes.

lessons.title: Petal Power
lessons.description: Use a helper function inside repeat(...) to make a flower pattern.
lessons.mission: Make a petal() helper, then repeat it to form a flower.
lessons.visualGoal: A flower-like pattern made from repeated petals.

milestones.title: Define petal()
milestones.description: Create a helper that draws one petal shape.
milestones.hint:
Try:
def petal():
    move(50)
    turn(60)
    move(50)
    turn(120)

milestones.title: Repeat your petal
milestones.description: Call petal() several times with a small turn between each one.
milestones.hint:
Try:
repeat(6):
    petal()
    turn(60)

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a petal() helper, then repeat it to form a flower.
milestones.hint: If it looks messy, lower your move distances and keep the turn between petals steady.

reward.title: Petal Power Badge
reward.flavor: You used a helper inside a loop to build a pattern.

lessons.hint: Define petal(), then call it inside repeat(...).
lessons.concept: Loops + functions let you build big patterns from small pieces.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("coral")
line_width(4)

def petal():
    move(50)
    turn(60)
    move(50)
    turn(120)

repeat(6):
    petal()
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: Petal power complete. You built a flower with a helper function.

lessons.title: Name a Number
lessons.description: Store a number in a variable so you can reuse it.
lessons.mission: Create a variable named side, then use it to draw a square.
lessons.visualGoal: A square made using move(side) instead of move(60).

milestones.title: Create a variable
milestones.description: Make a variable named side.
milestones.hint: Try: side = 60

milestones.title: Use your variable
milestones.description: Use side inside move(side).
milestones.hint: Inside the loop: move(side)

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Create a variable named side, then use it to draw a square.
milestones.hint: If you want a bigger square, change side = 60 to 100.

reward.title: Variable Wizard Badge
reward.flavor: You gave a number a name, then reused it.

lessons.hint: Use side = 60, then move(side) in your loop.
lessons.concept: Variables store values so you can reuse them.
lessons.starterCode:
side = 60
repeat(4):
    move(side)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: Variable success. Changing side now changes your whole square.

lessons.title: Two Sizes
lessons.description: Use two variables to make a pattern with different lengths.
lessons.mission: Create small and big, then draw a zigzag that uses both.
lessons.visualGoal: A zigzag with alternating short and long lines.

milestones.title: Create two variables
milestones.description: Make small and big variables.
milestones.hint: Try: small = 40\nbig = 110

milestones.title: Use both sizes
milestones.description: Draw some lines that use both small and big.
milestones.hint: Try move(small), then move(big).

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Create small and big, then draw a zigzag that uses both.
milestones.hint: If lines overlap, use more turns.

reward.title: Size Switcher Badge
reward.flavor: You used two named numbers to make a pattern.

lessons.hint: Make small and big, then alternate move(small) and move(big).
lessons.concept: Multiple variables help you organize different values.
lessons.starterCode:
small = 40
big = 110
move(small)
turn(60)
move(big)
turn(-120)
move(small)
turn(60)
move(big)
turn(-120)
move(small)
lessons.successMessage: Two sizes complete. You kept your numbers neat by naming them.

lessons.title: Color Variables
lessons.description: Store color names in variables so your drawing is easy to restyle.
lessons.mission: Create two color variables and switch between them while drawing.
lessons.visualGoal: A drawing with at least two color changes driven by variables.

milestones.title: Create color variables
milestones.description: Make c1 and c2 string variables.
milestones.hint: Try: c1 = "tomato"\nc2 = "teal"

milestones.title: Switch colors
milestones.description: Use pen_color(...) at least twice while drawing.
milestones.hint: Call pen_color(c1) first, then later pen_color(c2).

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Create two color variables and switch between them while drawing.
milestones.hint: Changing c1/c2 should restyle your whole drawing.

reward.title: Color Stylist Badge
reward.flavor: You restyled your drawing by changing just two variables.

lessons.hint: Make c1/c2, then pen_color(c1) and pen_color(c2).
lessons.concept: Variables can hold text too, like color names.
lessons.starterCode:
c1 = "tomato"
c2 = "teal"
line_width(8)
pen_color(c1)
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)
pen_color(c2)
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)
lessons.successMessage: Color variables complete. You used named colors instead of repeating strings.

lessons.title: If Color Choice
lessons.description: Use if/else to pick a color based on a variable.
lessons.mission: Make a variable named favorite, then use if/else to choose a pen_color.
lessons.visualGoal: A simple drawing where the color is chosen by if/else.
microHints: Add else: for the other color

milestones.title: Set favorite
milestones.description: Create a variable named favorite.
milestones.hint: Try: favorite = "teal"

milestones.title: Use if/else
milestones.description: Use an if statement and an else branch.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make a variable named favorite, then use if/else to choose a pen_color.
milestones.hint: After choosing the color, draw a short zigzag with move/turn.

reward.title: Decision Maker Badge
reward.flavor: You used if/else to choose what happens next.

lessons.hint: Pick a color in if/else, then draw.
lessons.concept: if/else lets code make a choice.
lessons.starterCode:
favorite = "teal"

if favorite == "teal":
    pen_color("teal")
else:
    pen_color("tomato")

move(60)
turn(60)
move(60)
turn(-120)
move(60)
lessons.successMessage: If success. You made a choice with code and then drew with it.

lessons.title: Two Paths
lessons.description: Change the path based on a variable.
lessons.mission: Make direction = "left" or "right" and use if/else to turn that way.
lessons.visualGoal: A path that turns left or right depending on your variable.
targetSteps: Use if/else to choose turn direction

milestones.title: Set direction
milestones.description: Create a variable named direction.
milestones.hint: Try: direction = "left"

milestones.title: Branch the turn
milestones.description: Use if/else to choose a different turn.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Make direction = "left" or "right" and use if/else to turn that way.
milestones.hint: Draw a few moves and turns so the difference is visible.

reward.title: Path Picker Badge
reward.flavor: You used a variable to pick between two paths.

lessons.hint: If/else chooses the turn direction.
lessons.concept: Branching lets one program behave in different ways.
lessons.successMessage: Two paths complete. Your code can choose a direction.

lessons.title: Rainbow Choice
lessons.description: Use elif to pick from more than two choices.
lessons.mission: Set pick = 1, 2, or 3 and choose a different pen_color for each.
lessons.visualGoal: A drawing where one of three colors is chosen.

milestones.title: Set pick
milestones.description: Create a variable named pick.
milestones.hint: Try: pick = 2

milestones.title: Add choices
milestones.description: Use if plus at least one elif, and end with else.

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Set pick = 1, 2, or 3 and choose a different pen_color for each.
milestones.hint: After choosing the color, draw a small repeating shape.

reward.title: Rainbow Brain Badge
reward.flavor: You made three choices with if/elif/else.

lessons.hint: Use if, elif, and else to pick a color.
lessons.concept: elif adds more choices than just if/else.
lessons.starterCode:
pick = 2
line_width(5)

if pick == 1:
    pen_color("tomato")
elif pick == 2:
    pen_color("goldenrod")
else:
    pen_color("royalblue")

repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)
lessons.successMessage: Rainbow choice complete. You gave your code three possible paths.

tracks.title: Explorer
tracks.checkpointPrompt: Build the drawing, then explain how the code structure made it easier to change.

lessons.title: Sequence and Side Effects
lessons.description: See how ordered commands produce visible output on the canvas.
lessons.mission: Draw a line and label it with text.
lessons.visualGoal: A simple drawn path with a text label attached to it.

reward.title: Sequence Scout Badge
reward.flavor: You connected ordered code to visible output.

lessons.hint: A single move plus write(...) is enough to prove the idea.
lessons.concept: Statements run in order and can create side effects on the canvas.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("slateblue")
move(120)
write("Sequence", 18)
lessons.successMessage: You demonstrated ordered execution with visible side effects.

lessons.title: Parameters as Inputs
lessons.description: Use different numbers to change movement results.
lessons.mission: Draw multiple lines with clearly different lengths.
lessons.visualGoal: Several lines whose lengths are visually distinct.
targetSteps: Use more than one move value

reward.title: Input Reader Badge
reward.flavor: You used numeric inputs to change output.

lessons.hint: Use different move distances so the output changes in a visible way.
lessons.concept: Arguments are inputs that shape runtime behavior.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("royalblue")
move(50)
turn(120)
move(90)
turn(120)
move(140)
lessons.successMessage: You used parameters to control how far the turtle moved.

lessons.title: Heading and Direction
lessons.description: Explore how turning changes the next path segment.
lessons.mission: Make a bent or zigzag path with several directional changes.
lessons.visualGoal: A multi-corner path instead of a straight line.
targetSteps: Move again in a new direction

reward.title: Heading Guide Badge
reward.flavor: You controlled orientation, not just distance.

lessons.hint: A sequence of move and turn commands will make the direction changes obvious.
lessons.concept: Heading determines the direction of the next movement.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("coral")
line_width(5)
move(60)
turn(60)
move(60)
turn(-120)
move(60)
turn(60)
move(60)
lessons.successMessage: You created a directional path by changing heading between segments.

lessons.title: Square from Sequence
lessons.description: Combine movement and turning into a predictable closed figure.
lessons.mission: Draw a square using repeated geometry.
lessons.visualGoal: A closed four-sided figure with matching sides.
targetSteps: Use the same move value each time

reward.title: Geometry Builder Badge
reward.flavor: You linked simple instructions to a complete shape.

lessons.hint: Try a loop rather than writing the same pair of commands four times.
lessons.concept: Simple sequences can create predictable geometric outcomes.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("tomato")
line_width(5)
repeat(4):
    move(90)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: You created a square through controlled repetition and turning.

lessons.title: Loops for Structure
lessons.description: Use a loop to organize repeated work into a smaller block of code.
lessons.mission: Build a repeated shape with repeat(...) instead of duplicated lines.
lessons.visualGoal: A loop-based shape with clean repeated sides.
targetSteps: Place the repeated instructions inside the loop

reward.title: Loop Logic Badge
reward.flavor: You used repetition as structure, not just convenience.

lessons.hint: A regular polygon is a simple way to test a loop.
lessons.concept: Loops reduce duplication while preserving consistent behavior.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(6):
    move(60)
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: You used a loop to organize repeated instructions clearly.

lessons.title: Polygon Comparisons
lessons.description: Change loop counts and turn angles to compare different regular shapes.
lessons.mission: Draw two different polygons in different positions.
lessons.visualGoal: Distinct loop-made shapes placed apart from one another.

reward.title: Polygon Analyst Badge
reward.flavor: You compared how loop settings affect shape geometry.

lessons.hint: A triangle and a hexagon make a strong contrast.
lessons.concept: Loop count and turning angle control polygon structure.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("salmon")
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(340, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("cornflowerblue")
repeat(6):
    move(55)
    turn(60)
lessons.successMessage: You compared two different polygons by changing loop structure.

lessons.title: Pattern Composition
lessons.description: Repeat a whole repeated shape to make a larger radial pattern.
lessons.mission: Create a spun pattern by drawing a shape, turning, and drawing it again.
lessons.visualGoal: A repeated motif arranged around the center.
targetSteps: Use one loop for the inner shape
Use another repeat for the overall pattern

reward.title: Pattern Architect Badge
reward.flavor: You built structure at more than one level.

lessons.hint: A square repeated around a center point is a clear first pattern.
lessons.concept: Layered repetition creates larger visual structures.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("coral")
line_width(3)
repeat(8):
    repeat(4):
        move(50)
        turn(90)
    turn(45)
lessons.successMessage: You composed a larger pattern by repeating an already repeated shape.

lessons.title: Color as State
lessons.description: Treat pen color as state that changes how later drawing commands behave.
lessons.mission: Draw a repeated pattern using at least three color changes.
lessons.visualGoal: A structured drawing with clear shifts in color state.

reward.title: State Painter Badge
reward.flavor: You changed drawing state to change the whole result.

lessons.hint: Reuse the same structure with different pen colors to make the changes obvious.
lessons.concept: State-changing commands affect what later instructions produce.
lessons.starterCode:
line_width(4)
pen_color("tomato")
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(260, 180)
pen_down()
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(360, 180)
pen_down()
pen_color("teal")
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: You changed color state intentionally and reused structure across the drawing.

lessons.title: Path vs Positioning
lessons.description: Separate movement that draws from movement that only repositions the cursor.
lessons.mission: Draw one shape, reposition without drawing, then draw a second shape.
lessons.visualGoal: Two shapes separated by space with no accidental connector line.

reward.title: Path Control Badge
reward.flavor: You separated drawing behavior from positioning behavior.

lessons.hint: Use pen_up(), go_to(...), and pen_down() to avoid connector lines.
lessons.concept: Drawing state and position state are related but distinct.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_color("cornflowerblue")
repeat(4):
    move(50)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(340, 210)
pen_down()
pen_color("coral")
repeat(3):
    move(60)
    turn(120)
lessons.successMessage: You managed path creation and positioning separately.

lessons.title: Coordinates and Placement
lessons.description: Use coordinates to place different forms intentionally around the canvas.
lessons.mission: Draw multiple shapes in clearly different positions.
lessons.visualGoal: Two or more shapes that are purposefully placed instead of clustered together.
targetSteps: Draw a different shape in each

reward.title: Placement Planner Badge
reward.flavor: You started composing with coordinates, not guesswork.

lessons.hint: Use absolute positions far enough apart that the layout becomes obvious.
lessons.concept: Coordinates support deliberate spatial composition.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_up()
go_to(150, 120)
pen_down()
pen_color("salmon")
repeat(3):
    move(70)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(360, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("cornflowerblue")
repeat(4):
    move(55)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: You used coordinates to control where each shape belonged.

lessons.title: Scene Composition
lessons.description: Combine placed shapes into a single readable scene.
lessons.mission: Build a simple scene with several distinct visual parts.
lessons.visualGoal: A composition that reads as one scene rather than separate test shapes.
targetSteps: Place them to read as one image

reward.title: Composition Badge
reward.flavor: You turned separate shapes into a coherent scene.

lessons.hint: A house-and-sun or tree-and-ground scene is a strong first composition.
lessons.concept: Composition emerges from placement, scale, and grouping.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_up()
go_to(190, 210)
pen_down()
pen_color("tomato")
repeat(4):
    move(80)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(190, 210)
pen_down()
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(3):
    move(80)
    turn(120)

pen_up()
go_to(380, 90)
pen_down()
pen_color("gold")
repeat(12):
    move(20)
    turn(30)
lessons.successMessage: You composed multiple parts into a single scene.

lessons.title: Drawing Order and Layering
lessons.description: Use ordering and color to make layered images feel more intentional.
lessons.mission: Create a scene where one shape acts like a background and later shapes sit over it.
lessons.visualGoal: A layered composition with visible foreground and background elements.
targetSteps: Draw a larger base shape first
Use color changes to strengthen the layering

reward.title: Layer Logic Badge
reward.flavor: You used drawing order to control visual hierarchy.

lessons.hint: Draw the broadest background form first and place smaller details later.
lessons.concept: Drawing order shapes what the viewer sees as foreground and background.
lessons.starterCode:
pen_up()
go_to(160, 170)
pen_down()
pen_color("skyblue")
repeat(4):
    move(140)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(210, 220)
pen_down()
pen_color("tomato")
repeat(4):
    move(70)
    turn(90)

pen_up()
go_to(330, 110)
pen_down()
pen_color("goldenrod")
repeat(12):
    move(18)
    turn(30)
lessons.successMessage: You used order and layering to make the composition clearer.

lessons.title: Defining Reusable Shapes
lessons.description: Use def to package a drawing routine into a reusable tool.
lessons.mission: Define square(size) and call it once.
lessons.visualGoal: A square drawn by calling a function you defined.

milestones.title: Define a helper
milestones.description: Create a function with def so you can reuse it.
milestones.hint:
Try:
def square(size):
    repeat(4):
        move(size)
        turn(90)

milestones.title: Call your helper
milestones.description: Call square(...) to draw at least one square.
milestones.hint: Try: square(80)

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Define square(size) and call it once.
milestones.hint: If your loop won't run, check the ':' after repeat(4): and your indentation.

reward.title: Helper Architect Badge
reward.flavor: You wrote your first reusable drawing function.

lessons.hint: Define square(size) once, then call it like square(80).
lessons.concept: Functions turn repeated structure into a named abstraction.
lessons.starterCode:
def square(size):
    repeat(4):
        move(size)
        turn(90)

square(80)
lessons.successMessage: You defined a helper function and reused it to draw a square.

lessons.title: Parameters for Reuse
lessons.description: Call the same function with different inputs to get different results.
lessons.mission: Draw two squares of different sizes in different places using square(size).
lessons.visualGoal: Two squares with clearly different sizes, separated on the canvas.

milestones.title: Keep square(size)
milestones.description: Define square(size) so it can be reused.
milestones.hint: Reuse your square(size) function from the previous lesson.

milestones.title: Place the second square
milestones.description: Use pen_up + go_to + pen_down so the squares are separated.
milestones.hint: Try: pen_up(); go_to(360, 210); pen_down()

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Draw two squares of different sizes in different places using square(size).
milestones.hint: Call square(...) twice with different numbers.

reward.title: Parameter Pilot Badge
reward.flavor: You changed the output by changing the input.

lessons.hint: Call square(40), then hop and call square(90).
lessons.concept: Parameters let one function create many related results.
lessons.starterCode:
def square(size):
    repeat(4):
        move(size)
        turn(90)

square(40)
pen_up()
go_to(360, 210)
pen_down()
square(90)
lessons.successMessage: You reused one function with two different inputs to draw two different squares.

lessons.title: Composing Helpers
lessons.description: Build bigger ideas by combining smaller helper functions.
lessons.mission: Define square(size) and house(size). Make house(size) call square(size). Then draw two houses.
lessons.visualGoal: Two house-like drawings built from helper functions.

milestones.title: Two helpers
milestones.description: Define square(size) and house(size).
milestones.hint: House can call square(size), then draw a triangle roof with move/turn.

milestones.title: Compose them
milestones.description: Make house(size) call square(size) inside it.
milestones.hint: Inside house(size): square(size)

milestones.title: Mission complete
milestones.description: Define square(size) and house(size). Make house(size) call square(size). Then draw two houses.
milestones.hint: Call house(...) twice, hopping between them with go_to(...) if needed.

reward.title: Composition Captain Badge
reward.flavor: You built a bigger idea out of smaller helpers.

lessons.hint: Let house(size) call square(size), then draw the roof.
lessons.concept: Composition is a powerful way to scale complexity without duplicating code.
lessons.starterCode:
def square(size):
    repeat(4):
        move(size)
        turn(90)

# Your house() can call square(size), then draw a roof.

def house(size):
    square(size)
    # Roof idea:
    # turn(45)
    # move(size * 0.7)
    # turn(90)
    # move(size * 0.7)
    # turn(45)

house(70)
pen_up()
go_to(360, 230)
pen_down()
house(90)
lessons.successMessage: Nice composition. You combined helpers to create a bigger drawing routine.

lessons.title: Variables as Names
lessons.description: Bind values to names so you can change behavior by changing one line.
lessons.mission: Create side = 80, then draw a square using move(side).
lessons.visualGoal: A square whose size is controlled by a variable.

reward.title: Name Binder Badge
reward.flavor: You bound a value to a name and reused it.

lessons.hint: Define side, then use move(side) inside repeat(4).
lessons.concept: Variables let you separate meaning (side length) from raw numbers.
lessons.starterCode:
side = 80
repeat(4):
    move(side)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: Variable bound. Changing side changes the whole shape.

lessons.title: Variables for Style
lessons.description: Store styling choices like color and width so you can restyle quickly.
lessons.mission: Create color_name and width, then draw with pen_color(color_name) and line_width(width).
lessons.visualGoal: A drawing whose look is controlled by variables.
targetSteps: Use them in pen_color(...) and line_width(...)

reward.title: Style System Badge
reward.flavor: You turned style into variables you can tweak.

lessons.hint: Try color_name = "tomato" and width = 6.
lessons.concept: Variables can hold configuration, making experiments easier.
lessons.starterCode:
color_name = "tomato"
width = 6
line_width(width)
pen_color(color_name)
repeat(4):
    move(60)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: Style variables complete. You can now restyle by changing just a couple lines.

lessons.title: Reassignment and Change
lessons.description: Change a variable to change what the program does later.
lessons.mission: Use one size variable, draw, change it, then draw again.
lessons.visualGoal: Two shapes with different sizes controlled by reassigning a variable.

reward.title: Reassignment Ranger Badge
reward.flavor: You changed a variable to change later behavior.

lessons.hint: Try size = 40, draw, then size = 90 and draw again.
lessons.concept: Reassignment updates a name to point to a new value for future statements.
lessons.starterCode:
def square(size):
    repeat(4):
        move(size)
        turn(90)

size = 40
square(size)
pen_up()
go_to(360, 210)
pen_down()
size = 90
square(size)
lessons.successMessage: Reassignment complete. You used the same name to drive two different results.

lessons.title: If Statements
lessons.description: Use if to run code only when a condition is true.
lessons.mission: Create steps = 6. If steps > 4, draw a hexagon using repeat(steps).
lessons.visualGoal: A polygon whose side count is controlled by a variable and an if statement.

reward.title: Branch Builder Badge
reward.flavor: You used if to control whether code runs.

lessons.hint: Try steps = 6 and if steps > 4: then repeat(steps): ...
lessons.concept: An if statement is a guard: code runs only when the condition is true.
lessons.starterCode:
steps = 6

if steps > 4:
    repeat(steps):
        move(55)
        turn(360 / steps)
lessons.successMessage: If statement complete. You guarded a drawing with a condition.

lessons.title: If/Else Branches
lessons.description: Add else so your program always chooses one of two paths.
lessons.mission: Create mode = "fast" or "slow". Use if/else to set step size, then draw a square.
lessons.visualGoal: A square whose size changes based on a mode variable.

reward.title: Two-Way Switch Badge
reward.flavor: You built two code paths and selected one.

lessons.hint: If mode == "fast" use a bigger size, else use a smaller one.
lessons.concept: if/else makes a decision with a fallback path.
lessons.starterCode:
mode = "fast"

if mode == "fast":
    size = 90
else:
    size = 50

repeat(4):
    move(size)
    turn(90)
lessons.successMessage: If/else complete. Your variable choice changed the whole output.

lessons.title: Elif Ladders
lessons.description: Use elif to handle multiple cases cleanly.
lessons.mission: Create level = 1, 2, or 3. Use if/elif/else to choose a color and line width, then draw a shape.
lessons.visualGoal: A drawing whose style changes based on a level variable.

reward.title: Case Handler Badge
reward.flavor: You handled multiple cases with elif.

lessons.hint: Pick different pen_color and line_width values in each branch.
lessons.concept: elif helps you avoid nested if statements by listing cases in order.
lessons.starterCode:
level = 2

if level == 1:
    pen_color("royalblue")
    line_width(3)
elif level == 2:
    pen_color("teal")
    line_width(6)
else:
    pen_color("tomato")
    line_width(9)

repeat(3):
    move(90)
    turn(120)
lessons.successMessage: Elif ladder complete. Your code handled multiple cases in a clean way.
